latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Magnets make invisible fields visible
latest.script_key: magnets can show a hidden force map watch the filings line up because each tiny piece becomes a small magnet in the field together they trace the direction of the force around the magnet the pattern is physics drawing itself which force next

latest.title: Octopus sits on a sea turtle like a hat
latest.script_key: that octopus is wearing a turtle tiny octopus arms tuck under the shell perfect grip zero weight that s why the turtle barely notices watch it glide like a living hat would you let one climb on you

latest.title: Lemurs eat leaves to survive not just cute
latest.script_key: lemurs eat leaves to survive watch how they strip the stem with their hands that s why they re always sniffing checking for toxic tannins you ever see a zoo lemur flop over and just nap after a big meal like that that s not laziness that s digestion working overtime would you eat something that makes you that tired

latest.title: Mountains aren’t as solid as they look
latest.script_key: watch this one twice those smooth snow patches they re sandpaper glaciers drag rocks that scrape mountains down not up that s why valleys look carved not built i love this one ever wonder why the alps aren t taller they re eroding faster than they grow would you survive a million years of this

latest.title: Mountains don't erode the way we think
latest.script_key: mountains don t erode the way we think those smooth slopes they re fake the real action is in the cracks watch how water carves them wider than we ever noticed that s why the tallest peaks stay sharp instead of rounding off did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Cells copy instructions before splitting
latest.script_key: cells have to copy themselves before dividing watch the cell boundary because division only works after the internal instructions are copied and organized then the cell can split the material into two working lives at this scale reproduction is careful logistics which tiny process next

latest.title: Elephants cool blood through giant ears

latest.title: Geysers make land faster than lava

latest.title: Llamas survive these mountains better than we do
latest.script_key: these llamas can t shiver so they run a different heater inside their feet watch how their toes get pink with blood right before they dig in that s why they can stand on ice like it s a hot sidewalk so if you ever hike up there

latest.title: Desert mountains fool your brain
latest.script_key: these mountains aren t solid rock see how the layers tilt different ways that s not erosion that s the land bending like hot taffy under its own weight the slowest force on earth isn t wind or water it s gravity pulling the crust down that s why valleys open where you least expect
